title: Hayflick Limit: The Science of Aging
script_summary: A concise explanation of the Hayflick limit and its implications on cellular aging.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you cells have a built-in lifespan?
This is the Hayflick limit—where cells can only divide about 50 times.
Every time they divide, they're inching closer to aging.
This limit stems from telomere shortening. When telomeres are too short, cells stop dividing.
Understanding this can unlock secrets to aging and health. But, we’re still learning.
Curious about how science can extend our lives? Hit that follow button!
